supersessions.notes: Uniformity principle carried forward unchanged; statement remains load-bearing.

v1 D4 framed executeAgent as phantom-tool-only single-turn dispatch. v2 rewrites to dual-mode dispatch (DEC-0004) with mode selection algorithm (DEC-0009) + agentic-mode tool execution through ExtensionContext (DEC-0008) + ExtensionContext as canonical tool-execution surface (DEC-0010). Both modes formally captured as MODE-001 (classifier) and MODE-002 (agentic). Replaces the implementation-hedge framing that capability-grant infrastructure (FEAT-004/005 + TASK-089/092) made dormant at agent dispatch tier.
v1 D1/D2/D3/D5/D6/D7 carried forward; D7 promoted from provisional to enacted. No substantive changes to the decisions themselves.

supersessions.v1_section_reference: v1 §6 Non-Goals 'add new features — only reorganizes and unifies existing capabilities'
supersessions.notes: v1 §6's 'no new features' framing was the framing under which the implementation hedge happened (extraction-only scope, no multi-tool dispatch). v2 §6 replaces with 'realizes the canonical body's commitments (FEAT-005/006/DEC-0047/TASK-091/JI-029) at the agent layer.' DEC-0004's dual-mode dispatch IS new framework capability relative to v1's extraction-only scope; explicitly so per canonical-body alignment.
